title: Avoid using value-based classes for locking in Java
description: This rule checks for the use of value-based classes for locking, which can lead to unpredictable results, contention, and deadlock issues.
recommendation: Avoid using value-based classes for locking. Use synchronization mechanisms designed for objects instead.
impact: Can lead to unpredictable results, contention, and deadlock issues.

checks.message: Avoid using value-based classes for locking. Use synchronization mechanisms designed for objects instead.
